metadata.title: Claude, Generated.
metadata.description: ai-asset-gen skill teardown: Higgsfield generation hub — image, video, 3D, audio, and virality analysis from one CLI. Nine model families, one decision tree, one --wait.

beats.narration_text: Dia dhuit, Liam. Today: ai-asset-gen — the Higgsfield generation hub. Image, video, three-D, audio, and virality analysis from one CLI. Nine model families, one decision tree. Liam, in for Bear.

props.runningText: * reading SKILL.md → picking model → building command → --wait

beats.narration_text: One skill, nine model families. G-P-T Image 2 for design and typography. Seedance 2.0 for cinematic video. Nano Banana for characters and cartoons. Marketing Studio for branded ads. Sonilo and Mirelo for audio. Multi-Image to 3D for meshes. Virality Predictor for scoring finished ads. One entry point: higgsfield generate create.

whatItDoes: Submit jobs to any Higgsfield model via the higgsfield CLI
9 model families: image / video / 3D / audio / analysis
--wait blocks the command until the result URL is printed
Chain with ai-character-id for face/identity consistency
phaseGates: Pick model: decision tree by task type (image/video/3D/audio/analysis)
Build command: prompt + media flags + params
Deliver: URL + one-line summary (model, duration if video)

beats.narration_text: Five steps. Bootstrap first — install the CLI and authenticate. Pick the model using the decision tree by task type. Build the command with the right flags: prompt, image, start-image, or video. Submit with --wait so it blocks until the URL prints. Deliver one URL, one line summary.

steps.label: Bootstrap
steps.detail: higgsfield CLI installed; account status OK

steps.label: Pick model
steps.detail: decision tree: image / video / 3D / audio / analysis

steps.label: Deliver
steps.detail: result URL + one-line summary (model, duration if video)

beats.narration_text: The model selection is the skill. Get it wrong and you pay for the wrong output. Here's the brief: a sixteen by nine editorial banner with typography. Which model?

props.runningText: * task type: image → checking brief → on-image text required

beats.narration_text: Self-demo: model selection decision tree — free, no Higgsfield call. Task: sixteen by nine editorial banner with typography. Step one: output type is image. Step two: the brief says high-fidelity, graphic design, on-image text. That locks in G-P-T Image 2 — the default for typography and design. Step three: one command with --wait. The decision tree is the skill. Higgsfield stays in the wallet until the model choice is locked.

codeLines: # 1. OUTPUT TYPE: image  (not video / 3D / audio / analysis)
# 2. BRIEF SIGNALS: high-fidelity, graphic design, on-image text
#    → GPT Image 2: default for design + typography ✓
# 3. COMMAND (model choice made — call happens on approval):
newsprint texture | terracotta + cream | bold condensed type" \
captionLines: Model selection is free. The Higgsfield API call is paid.
Lock the model choice before approving spend.

beats.narration_text: Law one: G-P-T Image 2 is the default image model for high-fidelity general generation, graphic design, U-I, banners, typography, and on-image text. On-image text is the decisive signal. Other models hallucinate typography. G-P-T Image 2 renders it. Reach for a specialist only when G-P-T Image 2 genuinely can't do the job.

props.quote: GPT Image 2 → default image model for high-fidelity general generation, graphic design, UI, banners, typography, and on-image text.
mechanismLines: On-image text requirement → GPT Image 2 (others hallucinate text)
Specialist models only when GPT Image 2 genuinely can't serve

beats.narration_text: Law two: pass --wait to generate create so the command blocks until done and prints the result URL itself. Avoid the two-step create-then-wait pattern. One command. One block. One URL. Bump --wait-timeout to twenty minutes for long video jobs. The --wait flag is the contract.

props.quote: Pass `--wait` to `generate create` so the command blocks until done and prints the result URL itself. Avoid the two-step `create` → `wait` pattern.
mechanismLines: --wait blocks the CLI until the job reaches terminal status
Result URL printed directly — no second 'wait' command needed
Avoid two-step: generate create → generate wait (fragile)
Bump --wait-timeout 20m for long video generation jobs

beats.narration_text: Law three: don't batch-ask. Pick a sane default model and ask one thing at a time — only if genuinely missing. If the brief is clear, pick the model, build the command, and submit. No model-selection dialogue. No pre-estimating cost. The UX rule is the interface: keep the human in the brief, not the model trivia.

props.quote: Don't batch-ask. Pick a sane default model and ask one thing at a time only if genuinely missing.
mechanismLines: Clear brief → pick model, build command, submit (no dialogue)
Never pre-estimate cost unless the user asks
One clarifying question only when info is genuinely missing
The skill owns model trivia; the human provides the brief

beats.narration_text: Claude, Generated. The artifact is one command: higgsfield generate create gpt-underscore-image-underscore-2, prompt, aspect ratio sixteen by nine, resolution two-k, --wait. Nine model families. One decision tree. One URL. The model selection is free. The generation is paid.

deliveryLines: Model selection is free; generation is paid
Default: GPT Image 2 · Seedance 2.0 · Nano Banana 2

beats.narration_text: Your turn. What needs generating — image, video, three-D, audio, or analysis? Pick your brief. Lock your model. Run the command. Liam, handing off to Bear.

props.runningText: * image / video / 3D / audio / analysis → pick your task

beats.narration_text: Claude, Generated. At NikBearBrown. Liam, in for Bear.
